title: Parallax whole-feature invariants manifest (v0.37 P0.3)
description: Declared at .parallax/<slug>/invariants.json by the /parallax:spec prohibition-reconciliation substep: the concrete cross-file invariant classes a per-slice green could still violate (PII/trust/anti-cheat exposure, money/pricing rules, shared-entity serialization, dead fields/seams, shared-interface breakage, mock-only I/O). scripts/feature-sweep.py executes it against the integrated tree before final completion. This is a CONCRETE-INVARIANT sweep, NOT a broad style/architecture review — every entry names a precise, checkable consequence.

forbidden_patterns.description: Patterns that must NOT appear in shipped code under `paths` (e.g. a PII field serialized into a response, a forbidden money literal). A match is a whole-feature violation.

required_consumers.description: A new/changed shared field must have a LIVE consumer — it is not 'covered' merely because a producer or a unit test mentions it. If `field` appears in `producer_paths` but nowhere in `consumer_paths`, it is a dead field/seam.

field.description: Literal token or Python regex of the shared field/identifier.

mock_only_slices.description: I/O-heavy slices whose unit tests are mock-only. Each must EITHER ship an integration/contract check matching `integration_glob` OR carry the visible `externals mocked -> integration unverified` stamp file; otherwise completion is blocked.

stamp.description: Repo-relative path to the explicit mock-only stamp file, e.g. .parallax/<slug>/stamps/<id>.mock-only
